name: Teretistridae
description: Teretistridae is a small family of passerine birds endemic to the island of Cuba. These warbler-like birds are found in forests and scrub habitats and are known for their distinctive vocalizations and active foraging behavior.

children.name: Teretistris
children.description: A genus of two warbler species endemic to Cuba, with plain grey-brown and yellow plumage.

children.name: Teretistris fernandinae
children.description: The Yellow-headed Warbler has a bright yellow head, grey upperparts, and pale underparts. It is found in scrub and woodland habitats across western and central Cuba.

children.name: Teretistris fornsi
children.description: The Oriente Warbler has a grey head, olive upperparts, and yellow underparts. It is endemic to eastern Cuba, inhabiting forests and scrub in the mountains and coastal regions.
